stemHtml: A pilot is taxiing out for take-off on a very hot day and his/her aircraft is equipped with carbon brakes. The pilot is aware that it is going to be a long taxi and wants to maximise the brakes' ability to absorb energy in case of a rejected take-off. To control the taxi speed, he/she decides to use:

options.a: Frequent light brake applications, as carbon-brake wear is determined by the number of times the brakes are applied.
options.b: Firm brake applications, as carbon-brake wear is determined by the number of times the brakes are applied.
options.c: Frequent light brake applications, as carbon-brake wear is determined by the brakes' temperature.
options.d: Firm brake applications, as carbon-brake wear is determined by the brakes' temperature.
